title: Pushing RC Car Project to GitHub

message.text:
I was making a repo for both project folders for my RC car project. Here is what I did in the terminal below. I'm trying to push it into my github but it won't create a repo. Can you check what I did wrong?

mongoose@mongoose:~$ cd /home/mongoose/Desktop/STM32_Workspace/RC_Car_Mark_I/
mongoose@mongoose:~/Desktop/STM32_Workspace/RC_Car_Mark_I$ git init
hint: Using 'master' as the name for the initial branch. This default branch name
hint: is subject to change. To configure the initial branch name to use in all
hint: of your new repositories, which will suppress this warning, call:
hint:
hint:   git config --global init.defaultBranch <name>
hint:
hint: Names commonly chosen instead of 'master' are 'main', 'trunk' and
hint: 'development'. The just-created branch can be renamed via this command:
hint:
hint:   git branch -m <name>
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/mongoose/STM32CubeIDE/workspace_1.19.0/RC_Car_Mark_I/.git/
mongoose@mongoose:~/Desktop/STM32_Workspace/RC_Car_Mark_I$ ls
rc_car_integrated_II  RC_Car_Transmitter_L432KC
mongoose@mongoose:~/Desktop/STM32_Workspace/RC_Car_Mark_I$ git status
On branch master

No commits yet

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
        .metadata/
        RC_Car_Transmitter_L432KC/
        rc_car_integrated_II/

nothing added to commit but untracked files present (use "git add" to track)
mongoose@mongoose:~/Desktop/STM32_Workspace/RC_Car_Mark_I$ git add .

mongoose@mongoose:~/Desktop/STM32_Workspace/RC_Car_Mark_I$ git push origin main
error: src refspec main does not match any
error: failed to push some refs to 'origin'

reply.text:
There are two issues:

1. **Branch name mismatch**: Your local branch is called `master` (from `git init`), but you're trying to push `main`.
2. **No commits**: You ran `git add .` but never committed the files.
3. **No remote**: You never added a remote origin URL or created the GitHub repo.

Here's what you need to do:

**Step 1**: Create the repo on GitHub first. You can use the GitHub CLI or create it on github.com. With the CLI:

```bash
gh repo create RC_Car_Mark_I --public --source=. --remote=origin
```

Or if you already created it on GitHub, add the remote manually:

```bash
git remote add origin https://github.com/eam95/RC_Car_Mark_I.git
```

**Step 2**: Commit your files and push:

```bash
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit: RC Car transmitter and integrated projects"
git branch -m master main
git push -u origin main
```

The key fixes:
- `git commit` was missing — you can't push without at least one commit
- `git branch -m master main` renames your branch from `master` to `main` to match what GitHub expects
- You need a remote (`origin`) pointing to your GitHub repo before pushing

Also, consider adding a `.gitignore` before committing to exclude build artifacts. Here's a good one for STM32CubeIDE projects:

```gitignore
# Build output
Debug/
Release/

# Eclipse/STM32CubeIDE metadata
.metadata/
.settings/
*.launch

# Compiled files
*.o
*.d
*.elf
*.bin
*.hex
*.map
*.list
```

turns.suggestedTitle: Pushing RC Car Project to GitHub
